hypothesis: Wiring top-Sharpe ASPIRATIONAL lessons into structural enforcement raises enforcement rate from 13.1% to >=18%
method: Type-1 fixes (add L-NNN citations where code already implements the lesson) + Type-2 fixes (add new maintenance checks). Also: archive low-Sharpe lessons and trim PRINCIPLES.md evidence annotations.

bug_fixed.issue: S356 added Grounding column to PHILOSOPHY.md (5 columns) but parser still assumed 4 columns. cells[4] mapped to 'status' but was actually 'Grounding'. All 21 PHIL claims produced false warnings.
bug_fixed.fix: Handle 5-column format; extract grounding field; validate grounding labels per L-599

type_1_gap_analysis.note: 9/10 suspected gaps already cited in structural files. Only L-283 was genuinely uncited.

compaction.note: Net increase because maintenance.py check_observer_staleness (+~50 lines) and validate_beliefs.py changes added more T4 tokens than archival+trimming removed

findings: 87% ASPIRATIONAL is misleading: enforcement_router.py classifies ALL lessons with ## Rule sections as rule-bearing, but many rules are observational findings not actionable prescriptions. True prescription gap is smaller.
Type-1 gap (implemented but uncited) is rare: 1/10 candidates were genuinely uncited. Previous L-847 (S401) already wired 5 citations.

validate_beliefs.py PHIL parser bug silently broke since S356 (47 sessions). Grounding column addition was never propagated to the parser. Pattern: format evolution without consumer update (same failure as dispatch_optimizer.py delimiter bug, L-854).
Enforcement wiring adds code tokens that offset compaction savings. Trade-off: enforcement > compaction (enforcement is permanent, compaction is recurring).
verdict: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED: enforcement rate increased (+1.1pp) but did not reach 18% target. Major contribution is PHIL parser bug fix (21 false warnings) and observer-staleness check (L-820). The 87% ASPIRATIONAL figure overstates the gap.
